findings.description: Auth pattern is correctly implemented and consistently applied across all five handlers (GET list, POST, GET single, PATCH, DELETE). Each handler independently calls `getServerSession(authOptions)`, checks for a null session (→ 401), then checks `session.user.role !== 'ADMIN'` (→ 403). This matches Decision 9 and the established pattern from `src/app/api/merchants/route.ts`. No auth bypass vectors observed.
findings.suggestion: No action needed. Consistent and correct.

findings.description: Protected field guard correctly queries the DB for `isProtected: true` fields and verifies each protected `fieldKey` exists in the incoming request body before executing the transaction. This prevents silent removal of the Company Name field via PATCH.
findings.suggestion: No action needed. Implementation is correct and matches the spec.

findings.description: Raw Prisma errors (stack traces, DB table names, constraint details) may be leaked in 500 responses because there is no try/catch around `prisma.formTemplate.create` (POST) or `prisma.$transaction` (PATCH). Although these are admin-only routes, leaking internal DB structure to any authenticated ADMIN user is a minor information disclosure risk.
findings.suggestion: Wrap Prisma calls in try/catch and return generic 500 messages on unexpected errors. Zod validation errors are already handled safely (returning `parsed.error.issues` for 400s is acceptable for admin routes).

findings.description: The `toFieldKey` function sanitizes user-supplied labels into a safe kebab-case key using `label.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-').replace(/(^-|-$)/g, '')`. This correctly strips all characters outside `[a-z0-9-]`, preventing fieldKey injection (e.g. SQL-like values, XSS payloads in keys). However, a label consisting entirely of non-alphanumeric characters (e.g. '!!!' or '---') would produce an empty string fieldKey `''` after stripping leading/trailing dashes.
findings.suggestion: Add a guard: if `toFieldKey(label)` returns an empty string, either fall back to a random short ID (e.g. `cuid()`) or return a 400 validation error. This prevents a fieldKey of `''` being stored in the DB, which could cause ambiguity in Task 3's submit endpoint lookups.

findings.description: The PATCH full-replacement uses Prisma's interactive transaction form (`prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {...})`), which is correctly specified over the array form to guarantee execution order. The delete-then-create pattern prevents partial states because Prisma's transaction rolls back on any error. `FieldResponse.fieldId` becomes null via `onDelete: SetNull` on the FK (defined in schema), so existing submissions are unaffected.
findings.suggestion: No action needed. Transaction strategy is correct per Decision in tech-spec.

findings.description: `buildZodSchema` is a pure function with no auth, DB access, or side effects. It correctly maps all 7 FieldType values. The `z.string().max(10000)` limit on TEXT/TEXTAREA values is present per Decision 10. EMAIL uses `z.string().email()` (Zod regex validation), URL uses `z.string().url()` — both are standard Zod validators with no known bypass vectors for their intended purpose.

DELETE correctly checks for template existence (404 if not found) before deleting. The cascade is handled by Prisma schema `onDelete: Cascade` on FormStep→FormField and `onDelete: SetNull` on FormSubmission→templateId. No direct SQL used — no SQL injection surface.

These admin management routes (`GET /api/templates`, `POST /api/templates`, `GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/templates/[id]`) are NOT in the middleware allowlist per Decision 7. The middleware allowlist only permits exact public paths (`/api/templates/:id/public`, `/api/templates/:id/submit`). Admin routes remain behind NextAuth session middleware, providing defense-in-depth in addition to the per-handler role checks.
findings.suggestion: No action needed. Middleware configuration (not modified in this task) is expected to remain correct per Decision 7.

summary: All admin routes correctly implement session + role checks with no observed bypass vectors. The protected field guard is properly implemented. No SQL injection surface (all DB access via Prisma ORM with parameterized queries). The main security-relevant finding is SA-04 (empty fieldKey from all-symbol label) and SA-03 (raw error leakage on 500s). Both are low severity given these are admin-only endpoints. No critical or high-severity security issues found. Status: approved with low-severity recommendations.
